title: bupleurum marschallianum
intro: Bupleurum marschallianum, also known as Marshall's knotted hedge parsley or knotted chervil, is a perennial plant in the family Apiaceae, native to eastern Asia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving stress and anxiety, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections. Bupleurum marschallianum is often used in herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or used topically to promote health and well-being.
